name: Dyson Airwrap Multi-Styler Complete Long (Vinca Blue/Rosé)

specs.Attachments: 6 attachments — barrels (1 in, 1.2 in × 2 for both curl directions), smoothing brushes (oval, paddle), pre-styling dryer
specs.Technology: Coanda effect uses air to attract and wrap hair around barrel — no extreme heat
specs.Heat Setting: Max 150°C — significantly lower than conventional tools
specs.Hair Types: Damp and dry hair — works on fine, medium, and thick hair

whatWorks: The Coanda effect — where fast-moving air creates suction that draws objects toward a surface — is what makes the Airwrap fundamentally different from every other curl tool. Hair wraps around the barrel via airflow rather than being clamped at extreme heat, which means you're styling with 150°C instead of the 230°C+ most curling irons require. Less heat means less damage over years of use.

Replacing three separate tools (hair dryer, curling iron, straightening brush) with one device that lives in a case has real daily-life convenience value. The attachment swap takes 5 seconds, and the storage case keeps everything organized — this matters if you travel with your styling tools.

The long barrel (1.2 inch) creates loose, natural-looking waves that most curling irons struggle to replicate without leaving a kink at the end. The Airwrap's results consistently look more effortless than clip-curling-iron results because there's no tool mark where the clamp holds the hair.

Results on fine and medium hair are exceptional — the Coanda effect works best when it can attract lighter hair easily. Users with fine hair report salon-quality curls that last all day. Thick hair works too but may need longer hold time on the barrel.
worthKnowing: The $699 CAD price is the most significant drawback. For that money you could buy a professional curling iron, a salon-quality blow dryer, and a quality straightening brush separately. The Airwrap justifies its cost for daily users who value low heat damage, convenience, and storage — but it's a luxury purchase.

The learning curve is real, especially for beginners. Hair needs to be damp (not wet, not dry) for optimal results on the curl barrels. The technique of getting hair to wrap consistently takes most people 2–3 weeks of practice before it becomes intuitive.

Hair wrapping direction requires different barrel orientation for left-side and right-side curls. The Complete Long set includes two versions of each barrel size for both directions, which eliminates fumbling — but you need to consciously use the right barrel for the section you're styling.

Very thick or coarse hair may not get consistent Coanda attraction and could require working in smaller sections than finer hair types. Dyson's results are best documented on fine to medium hair; thick hair users should watch unsponsored reviews for their specific hair type before purchasing.

commonComplaints.title: Expensive and a steep learning curve…
commonComplaints.body: Took me a solid month before I could get consistent results. The YouTube tutorials help but you really do have to practice. The results when you get it right are worth it, but it's not plug-and-play.

commonComplaints.title: Doesn't work well on thick coarse hair…
commonComplaints.body: My hair is really thick and coarse. The hair doesn't wrap onto the barrel easily — it takes effort and multiple tries. The results are okay but I expected more given the price.

commonComplaints.title: Curls don't hold all day for my hair type…
commonComplaints.body: I have straight heavy hair that falls fast. The Airwrap curls look beautiful but they drop out by noon. I need to use hairspray to make them last which isn't ideal.

commonComplaints.title: Case is bulky for travel…
commonComplaints.body: The tool and attachments case is nice looking but it takes up half a carry-on. Traveling with all six attachments means sacrificing real luggage space.

verdict: The Dyson Airwrap is a genuinely innovative hair tool — not a gimmick — that delivers salon-quality curls and waves at meaningfully lower temperatures than any curling iron. The combination of low heat damage, multi-tool versatility, and results quality justifies the $699 CAD price for the right user: someone who styles their hair regularly, values long-term hair health, and will invest time in learning the technique. For occasional use or users who can't spend 2–3 weeks learning the system, it's not the right spend.

realBuyers.quote: I've colored and heat-styled my hair for 15 years. Since switching to the Airwrap my hair has so much more shine and my split ends are much better. The lower heat is real.
My hair has never looked this good. Once you figure out the technique (watch tutorials!) the results look like a professional blowout every morning.
Worth every cent. I use it every day and it replaced my dryer, two curling irons, and a straightening brush. My morning routine is actually faster now.
I gift one to myself every 5 years. The first one lasted 7 years of daily use before showing any wear. That puts the cost in perspective.

sources.title: Dyson Airwrap Multi-Styler Complete Long — Amazon.ca

metaDescription: Dyson Airwrap Multi-Styler Complete Long review for Canada: Coanda curling at 150°C, 6 attachments, replaces 3 tools. ~$699 CAD on Amazon.ca. Honest on learning curve.
introText: The Dyson Airwrap Multi-Styler Complete Long uses Coanda airflow technology to curl, wave, and smooth hair at a maximum 150°C — well below the 230°C+ of conventional tools — replacing your dryer, curling iron, and brush in one device. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B0CLQPN1DL) at around $699 CAD.
